question_title: Which lab will have the highest score on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index on August 31, 2026?
summary: Anthropic is the clear status-quo leader on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index as of early July 2026, holding both #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60) and #2 (Opus 4.8, ~56), roughly 5 points ahead of the nearest rival. Over the ~2-month horizon to Aug 31, the main risk to Anthropic is the index's frequent reordering — OpenAI briefly led in April 2026 before Fable 5 retook #1 in June — meaning whoever ships the newest frontier model often takes the top spot. OpenAI (~55) is the strongest challenger; Google (in 'Other', ~50) is a credible dark horse; Z AI (GLM-5.2, ~52) is ~8 points back and unlikely to be outright #1 in two months. Any challenger must ship a model scoring above ~60 within the window. I keep meaningful probability across all four options given release-timing uncertainty, while favoring Anthropic.

factors.description: Anthropic holds both #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60/64.9) and #2 (Opus 4.8, ~56), ~5 pts ahead; freshest top model (launched June 9, 2026). Any challenger must exceed ~60 to overtake.
OpenAI is closest rival (~55, GPT-5.5/5.6) and historically leapfrogs — briefly held #1 in April 2026; likely to ship a new frontier model within 2 months.
AA Index reorders frequently; over 2 months the leader is often whoever ships newest — adds uncertainty against the status-quo leader.
Z AI (GLM-5.2, ~52) is ~8 pts behind #1; fast-moving open lab but a large gap to close to reach outright #1 in 2 months.
'Other' includes Google DeepMind (~50, could ship a higher Gemini), xAI, DeepSeek, Alibaba — collectively a real leapfrog chance.

reasoning:
Forecast submitted.

**Which lab will lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index on Aug 31, 2026?**

- **Anthropic: 50%** — Current clear leader, holding both #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60) and #2 (Opus 4.8), ~5 points ahead. Freshest top model. Any rival must ship a model scoring above ~60 to overtake.
- **OpenAI: 28%** — Strongest challenger (~55), historically leapfrogs (briefly led in April 2026), likely to ship a new frontier model within the window.
- **Other: 15%** — Google DeepMind (~50) is a credible dark horse; also xAI, DeepSeek, Alibaba.
- **Z AI: 7%** — GLM-5.2 (~52) is credible but ~8 points back, unlikely to reach outright #1 in two months.

The key uncertainty is release timing: over a 2-month horizon on a frequently-reordering leaderboard, the top spot often goes to whoever ships the newest frontier model — which is why meaningful probability stays on the challengers despite Anthropic's current lead.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the Creator with the highest Intelligence Index score of an AI model by the Artificial Analysis [LLM Leaderboard](https://artificialanalysis.ai/leaderboards/models) when checked by Metaculus on or after August 31, 2026.
fine_print: Please note that this question resolves based on what is displayed at the resolution source, regardless of any changes to methodology. In case of a tie or the highest score being a lab not listed in the other options, the question resolves as Other.

factors.description: Anthropic currently holds #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60/64.9) AND #2 (Opus 4.8), ~5 pts ahead of nearest rival. Freshest top model (launched June 9, 2026).
OpenAI is closest rival (~55, GPT-5.5/5.6) and historically leapfrogs — briefly held #1 in April 2026. Likely to ship a new frontier model within 2 months.
Leaderboard reorders frequently; 2-month horizon means whoever ships newest top model likely wins. Adds uncertainty against status-quo leader.
Z AI (GLM-5.2, ~52) is ~8 pts behind #1; fast-moving open lab but a large gap to close to reach outright #1 in 2 months.
Other includes Google DeepMind (~50, could ship Gemini 3.5 Pro/4.0), xAI, DeepSeek, Alibaba — collectively a real chance of a leapfrog release.

input.anchor: Status-quo leader (Anthropic) base rate for holding #1 over a 2-month volatile-leaderboard horizon: ~45-55%.
input.assessment: Anthropic is the clear status-quo leader with a ~5-point lead and the two most recent top models. But the AA Index reorders frequently (GPT-5.5 led in April, Fable 5 in June), and over a 2-month horizon the leader is often whoever ships the newest frontier model. OpenAI is the strongest challenger. Z AI is credible but 8 points back — unlikely to be outright #1. Google (in 'Other') is a serious dark horse. I keep meaningful probability on all four options.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether OpenAI or Google ships a new frontier model before Aug 31 that leapfrogs Fable 5. Release timing is the dominant unknown.
input.tool_audit: research() returned a rich, well-sourced snapshot of the leaderboard with multiple corroborating sources (AA, DataLearner, BenchLM). Sufficient; no failures. Note the environment is a simulated 2026 with fictional model names, taken at face value.
input.process_reflection: The research tool gave exactly what was needed for a leaderboard-standings question in one call. For MC 'who leads a fast-moving leaderboard' questions, the main uncertainty is unmodelable release timing — hard to reduce with more research.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified all five factors against the research trace and the worldview store.

GROUNDING — all factor core claims are supported by the single research() call:
- Factor 1 (Anthropic #1 Fable 5 ~60/64.9, #2 Opus 4.8, ~5-pt lead, June 9 launch): fully confirmed by trace data_points and the AA launch-article source.
- Factor 2 (OpenAI ~55 GPT-5.5/5.6, briefly held #1 April 2026): confirmed — trace cites the AA article "GPT-5.5 tops the Index by 3 points" (Apr 23, 2026) and DataLearner GPT-5.6 at 55.
- Factor 3 (leaderboard reorders frequently): confirmed by research caveat.
- Factor 4 (GLM-5.2 ~52, ~8 pts back): confirmed (52 vs 60).
- Factor 5 (Other: Google ~50, xAI, DeepSeek, Alibaba): grounded. Minor note — research shows Gemini 3.5 *Flash* at 50; the factor's "Gemini 3.5 Pro/4.0" is forward-looking speculation, not a claimed current fact, so acceptable.

No hallucination. No wrong-direction factor (Factor 3's +0.3 for OpenAI is coherent: volatility aids challengers). Factors 2 and 3 mildly overlap (both [OpenAI], both lean on the April→June reorder) but are distinct enough not to be double-counting.

RESOLUTION CRITERIA: Provided and clear (highest Intelligence Index Creator on AA leaderboard on/after Aug 31; tie/unlisted → Other). Factors and assessment engage the correct Creator-level, score-based terms and the Other bucket. No recovery needed.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: None. Anthropic's June lead is used as status-quo/base-rate evidence, forward-looking to Aug 31. No Case-2 violation.

TOOL AUDIT: Agent claims "no failures"; ground truth confirms 0/10 errors. Sources named (AA, DataLearner, BenchLM) all appear in the trace. Accurate.

COUNTERARGUMENT: Strong and genuine — constructs a coherent alternative (index volatility + OpenAI/Google ship cadence + stale June lead → OpenAI+Other combined exceed Anthropic, pushing it below 45%). Not a token gesture.

WHY WARN — cross-question worldview signal ignored: The sibling worldview forecast created the same day ("Will OpenAI make GPT-5.6 GA before July 12", state=fresh) states as fact that "Anthropic's Fable5/Mythos5 were pulled back by the US govt" under government-clearance gating. The current forecast's research trace never mentions this. It's materially relevant context — the same government-gating dynamic also constrains OpenAI's GPT-5.6 shipping cadence (a pillar of the OpenAI-challenger case). This is a warn-level omission rather than forecast-flipping, because resolution is score-based (a gated model's score can still display on the leaderboard) rather than availability-based, so direction is largely insulated.

PROBABILITY: I independently estimate Anthropic slightly higher (~52-55%) than the agent's 47%, because Anthropic holds BOTH top slots with a robust 5-point lead and any challenger must ship a model scoring above ~60 within 2 months (OpenAI is 5 back, Google 10 back, Z AI 8 back). However, the volatility evidence (GPT-5.5→Fable 5 reordering on a ~2-month cadence) is real and the agent's own anchor was 45-55%. The ~5-8pp gap is within a defensible range and does not rise to "meaningfully wrong." The OpenAI(29)/Other(17)/Z AI(7) split is reasonable. No fail on probability.

Net: well-grounded, correctly scoped to resolution criteria, honest self-examination; the only substantive defect is the un-integrated worldview signal about Anthropic's government pull-back.
